title: Zaluzianskya diffusa
taxonomy: Zaluzianskya diffusa is classified within the family of Scrophulariaceae. This species belongs to the genus Zaluzianskya, which is a group of flowering plants native to Southern Africa. The specific epithet 'diffusa' suggests its spreading habit, but further information would be needed to confirm its full botanical classification. Zaluzianskya diffusa is often included in the family Orobanchaceae by some taxonomic systems, as it shares characteristics with the broomrape family. However, its classification can vary depending on the source and current taxonomic understanding.

intro: Zaluzianskya diffusa, also known as evening primrose, is a flowering plant species native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic benefits, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Zaluzianskya diffusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
